Title: The MRE11–ATM–SOG1 DNA damage signaling pathway confers rice immunity to Xanthomonas oryzae

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis, comet assay, gamma-H2AX immunostaining, disease lesion assay, metabolite profiling, immunofluorescence, in vitro interaction/competition assay with TOP6A3
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of KU70 enhances resistance to Xoo and increases/accelerates phenolic phytoalexin accumulation by relieving repression of ATM signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ku70 mutants are more resistant to Xoo than WT. 2. ku70 mutants accumulate higher levels of several phenolic phytoalexins after irradiation or Xoo infection. 3. KU70 normally restrains alt-NHEJ/HR and limits ATM-mediated immune signaling.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct use is limited because ku70 causes sporophytic sterility; serves as mechanistic target mimicked by TOP6 overexpression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial phytoalexins contributing to bacterial blight resistance
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Sporophytic sterility reported previously and noted here as breeding limitation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it functions as a DSB repair factor rather than enzyme or TF. Loss of KU70 increased DSB-associated gamma-H2AX and comet signals and promoted resistant phenotype.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by gamma irradiation and Xoo infection downstream of MRE11-ATM-SOG1; likely contributes to phytoalexin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os01g0897600 is strongly induced after gamma irradiation and Xoo infection. 2. Its induction is reduced in mre11, atm-3, and sog1 mutants. 3. SOG1 directly binds its promoter.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for boosting phenylpropanoid-derived phytoalexins, but enzyme identity was not specified in the paper text provided
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper did not provide clear gene symbol/enzyme assignment in the supplied text.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by gamma irradiation and Xoo infection downstream of MRE11-ATM-SOG1; likely contributes to phytoalexin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os07g0677600 is upregulated by irradiation and Xoo infection. 2. Its induction depends on MRE11, ATM, and SOG1. 3. SOG1 directly binds its promoter.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream engineering target for increasing phytoalexin flux; exact enzyme identity not stated in provided text
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific molecular function not resolved in the provided article text.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by gamma irradiation and Xoo infection downstream of MRE11-ATM-SOG1; likely contributes to phytoalexin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os04g0688300 is induced by irradiation and Xoo infection. 2. Its induction is compromised in mre11, atm-3, and sog1 mutants. 3. SOG1 directly associates with its promoter.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for phytoalexin enhancement once exact enzymatic role is confirmed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific enzyme/gene symbol not explicitly named in supplied text.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Induced by gamma irradiation and Xoo infection downstream of MRE11-ATM-SOG1; likely contributes to phytoalexin biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Os03g0235000 is induced after irradiation and Xoo infection. 2. Its induction requires MRE11, ATM, and SOG1. 3. SOG1 directly binds its promoter.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream target for strengthening phytoalexin biosynthesis, pending exact annotation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Leaves induced at 6 h after irradiation and 48 h after Xoo infection
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite pathway membership, exact enzyme name is not explicitly provided in the supplied text, so retained as Common Gene rather than Pathway Gene.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Upstream activation of ATM-dependent DNA damage signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Induction of phenylpropanoid pathway and downstream phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis during pathogen-triggered DNA damage response
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ATM signaling module; downstream SOG1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive upstream requirement for induction of phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes via ATM-SOG1
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Xanthomonas oryzae pv. oryzae infection (ZJ173); gamma irradiation-induced DNA double-strand breaks
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: MRE11–ATM–SOG1 pathway; discussed as DNA double-strand break sensor module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant (mre11), RNAi, lesion-length disease assay, RT-qPCR of downstream phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of rice resistance to Xoo and required for induction of phytoalexin-related phenylpropanoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential upstream activator of DNA damage-triggered phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial phytoalexins contributing to resistance against bacterial blight pathogen Xoo
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: mre11 mutant showed slowed growth at post-germination stage
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Direct loss of MRE11 is not desirable because mre11 mutant showed growth defects; useful as mechanistic component of immunity pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MRE11 is required for full resistance to Xoo in rice. 2. Loss of MRE11 reduces induction of phenylpropanoid pathway genes after gamma irradiation or Xoo infection. 3. MRE11 acts upstream of ATM-SOG1 to promote phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MRE11RNAi line did not show clear resistance phenotype, indicating partial knockdown was insufficient in this study.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: GLA4; K3; ZH11 background lines used
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: atm-1, atm-2, atm-3 mutants in GLA4/K3/ZH11 backgrounds
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Activation of phenylpropanoid pathway and phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis under DNA double-strand break conditions
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SOG1 phosphorylation, enabling transcriptional induction of phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Xoo (ZJ173)-induced DNA double-strand breaks; bleomycin; gamma irradiation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: atm mutants, disease lesion assays, immunoblot for SOG1 phosphorylation, wortmannin inhibition, metabolite profiling by LC-MS/HPLC, RT-qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes resistance to Xoo by enabling biosynthesis of phenolic phytoalexins; loss of ATM causes susceptibility and reduced phytoalexin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Indispensable positive controller of DNA damage-induced phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Xoo (ZJ173) infection; gamma irradiation; bleomycin; wortmannin
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: atm mutants are more susceptible to Xoo; no major vegetative growth defect reported for atm-2/atm-3 lines in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Acts downstream of TOP6-facilitated engineering strategy; preserving/enhancing ATM signaling is promising for bacterial blight resistance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ATM, but not ATR, positively regulates rice resistance to Xoo. 2. ATM is required for SOG1 phosphorylation after bleomycin and Xoo infection. 3. ATM is indispensable for induction of phenolic phytoalexins under gamma irradiation and Xoo infection.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ATM was genetically downstream of TOP6A3 because atm-2 TOP6A3-OE1 resembled atm-2 in susceptibility.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: ATM-dependent phosphorylation and direct transcriptional activation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional activation of phenylpropanoid biosynthetic genes required for phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates these phenylpropanoid pathway genes by binding promoter CT(N)7AAG motifs
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ATM-mediated DNA damage signaling triggered by Xoo infection, bleomycin, or gamma irradiation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression (SOG1-FLAG-OE1), mutant analysis, immunoblot detection of phosphorylation, ChIP-qPCR, RT-qPCR, disease lesion assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Phosphorylated SOG1 directly activates phenylpropanoid genes, promotes phenolic phytoalexin accumulation, and enhances resistance to Xoo
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional activator linking DNA damage signaling to phytoalexin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial phytoalexins involved in bacterial blight defense
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SOG1-FLAG-OE1 displayed normal vegetative growth in this study
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Positive regulator for engineering stronger phytoalexin-based resistance, though pathway was presented mainly as mechanistic target via TOP6 facilitation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SOG1 is phosphorylated in an ATM-dependent manner after DNA damage and Xoo infection. 2. SOG1 directly binds promoters of four phenylpropanoid genes. 3. SOG1 overexpression enhances resistance, while sog1 mutation compromises resistance and gene induction.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes phosphorylated SOG1 (SOG1-p) as the active form connecting DNA damage signaling to metabolic gene activation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: DNA repair pathway regulator/topoisomerase VI complex subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Represses KU70 loading at DSBs, shifting c-NHEJ toward alt-NHEJ and HR, thereby amplifying ATM signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Indirect enhancement of phenylpropanoid pathway activation and phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis via DSB repair pathway remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: KU70 loading at DSB sites; downstream ATM-SOG1 pathway
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly enhances SOG1-regulated phenylpropanoid gene activation by increasing ATM signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: TOP6 complex with TOP6B; interacts with KU70
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression line, EMSA/in vitro DNA binding competition assay, immunofluorescence for TOP6A3/LIG4/COM1/RAD51, disease assay, metabolite profiling, genetic epistasis with atm-2
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression enhances resistance to Xoo and increases/accelerates phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis without obvious yield penalty
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent amplifier of phytoalexin accumulation through remodeling DSB repair hierarchy and strengthening ATM signaling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antimicrobial defense metabolites against bacterial blight
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: TOP6A3 foci strongly induced after Xoo infection in mesophyll nuclei
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpression line showed few nuclear foci under control and strong nuclear loading after Xoo infection
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hypersensitive post-germination phenotype after seed irradiation; earlier heading/harvest, but no obvious yield or photosynthesis penalty under normal growth
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising breeding/engineering target to enhance bacterial blight resistance without major fitness cost
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOP6A3 overexpression represses KU70 recruitment and shifts DSB repair from c-NHEJ to alt-NHEJ/HR. 2. This strengthens ATM-dependent signaling and increases phenolic phytoalexin accumulation after Xoo infection. 3. TOP6A3 overexpression confers enhanced Xoo resistance with little apparent growth or yield penalty.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not effective for resistance to Magnaporthe oryzae BR22 in this study; atm-2 abolishes TOP6A3-OE1 resistance, placing ATM downstream.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: DNA repair pathway regulator/topoisomerase VI complex subunit
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Facilitates TOP6A3-mediated repression of KU70/c-NHEJ and enhancement of ATM signaling
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Indirect promotion of phenolic phytoalexin biosynthesis through DSB repair pathway remodeling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: TOP6 complex function affecting KU70/c-NHEJ and downstream ATM signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirect positive effect on SOG1-regulated phenylpropanoid pathway via enhanced ATM signaling
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression line, disease assay, irradiation response, HR-related assays
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression confers greater resistance to Xoo and supports the TOP6-facilitated ATM immune pathway
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Supportive amplifier of phytoalexin-promoting ATM pathway through TOP6 complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hypersensitive post-germination phenotype after seed irradiation; normal vegetative and reproductive growth; no obvious yield penalty reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding bacterial blight resistance with minimal fitness costs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TOP6B overexpression enhances resistance to Xoo. 2. TOP6B acts with TOP6A3 in the TOP6 complex to promote ATM-mediated immunity. 3. Overexpression did not cause obvious growth or yield penalties under normal conditions.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Metabolite-level evidence was stronger for TOP6A3-OE1 than TOP6B-OE1, but TOP6B was still a core experimentally tested component of the proposed breeding strategy.
